title: TipSubmission
description: A confidential tip, complaint, or information referral submitted to the Brooklyn DA — the everyday inbound transaction (report a crime concern, a fraud, an unsafe landlord, a hate incident, or information about an open matter). NET-NEW: there is NO public API and NO Open Data twin. Today the only inbound paths are a reCAPTCHA/hCaptcha-gated WordPress contact form, a phone hotline, and bureau email addresses. This schema serves both the write surface (submit a tip) and a minimal read surface (check the status of a tip you submitted, by reference number).

x-source.form: WordPress contact form (reCAPTCHA/hCaptcha per Permissions-Policy header)

tipId.description: Server-assigned confidential reference number. Omit on create; returned on the created resource so the submitter can check status.

status.description: Lifecycle status, set by the office — never by the submitter.

category.description: What the tip is about, so it routes to the right bureau/unit.

summary.description: Free-text description of the tip / concern.

incidentDate.description: When the incident occurred, if known.

emergency.description: If true, the submitter is directed to call 911 immediately — this channel is not for emergencies in progress.

anonymous.description: Whether the submitter wishes to remain anonymous. If true, contact fields may be omitted.

contact.description: Optional submitter contact details (may be omitted for anonymous tips).

consentToContact.description: Whether the office may follow up with the submitter.
